questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The President of India is elected by an electoral college consisting of the elected members of both Houses of Parliament and the Legislative Assemblies of States.
questions.reason: Reason (R): This provision helps to ensure that the President is accountable to the elected representatives of the people.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The electoral college system is designed to ensure that the President is chosen by a representative body, rather than directly by the people.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The Vice-President of India is ex-officio Chairman of the Rajya Sabha.
questions.reason: Reason (R): As per Article 69 of the Constitution, the Vice-President is to preside over the Rajya Sabha, but he has no vote in the event of a tie.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The Vice-President's role in the Rajya Sabha is specified in Article 69, and the provision regarding voting in the event of a tie is also mentioned in the same article.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The President of India can nominate 12 members to the Rajya Sabha.
questions.reason: Reason (R): According to Article 80 of the Constitution, the total number of members in the Rajya Sabha is 245, out of which 12 members are to be nominated by the President.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Article 80 specifies the composition of the Rajya Sabha, including the number of nominated members.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The President of India serves for a fixed term of five years.
questions.reason: Reason (R): As per Article 56 of the Constitution, the President holds office for a term of five years from the date of assumption of office.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Article 56 specifies the term of the President's office and the date from which it begins.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The President of India can dissolve the Lok Sabha.
questions.reason: Reason (R): As per Article 85 of the Constitution, the Lok Sabha can be dissolved by a Proclamation issued by the President after obtaining the advice of the Council of Ministers.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The President cannot dissolve the Lok Sabha unilaterally; Article 85 specifies the procedure for issuing a Proclamation.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The Vice-President of India is also the ex-officio Chairman of the Planning Commission.
questions.reason: Reason (R): As per Article 74 of the Constitution, the Vice-President is the ex-officio Chairman of the Planning Commission, but the actual work is done by the Planning Commission Secretariat.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The Vice-President is not the actual Chairman of the Planning Commission; the Planning Commission Secretariat carries out the actual work.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The President of India has the power to appoint the Chief Justice of India.
questions.reason: Reason (R): As per Article 124(2) of the Constitution, the President appoints the Chief Justice of India, who is then the senior-most judge of the Supreme Court.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Article 124(2) specifies the President's role in appointing the Chief Justice of India.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The President of India can appoint judges to the Supreme Court.
questions.reason: Reason (R): As per Article 124(3) of the Constitution, the President appoints judges to the Supreme Court after consulting with the Chief Justice of India and the other two senior-most judges of the Supreme Court.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Article 124(3) specifies the President's role in appointing judges to the Supreme Court and the consultation process with the Chief Justice and other judges.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The President of India has the power to grant pardons, reprieves, and remissions.
questions.reason: Reason (R): As per Article 72 of the Constitution, the President has the power to grant pardons, reprieves, and remissions, but this power is limited to certain cases.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Article 72 specifies the President's power to grant pardons, reprieves, and remissions, and the limitations on this power.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The President of India is the Head of the Union Executive.
questions.reason: Reason (R): As per Article 53 of the Constitution, the President is the Head of the Union Executive, who is responsible for appointing the Council of Ministers.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Article 53 specifies the President's role as the Head of the Union Executive and the responsibility to appoint the Council of Ministers.
